title: BES Cyber System Categorization
text: Identifies and categorizes BES Cyber Assets and BES Cyber Systems. R1 requires categorization by impact level: HIGH IMPACT (control centers + backup with > 3000 MW), MEDIUM IMPACT (generation > 1500 MW, transmission > 500 kV, control centers for medium), LOW IMPACT (other BES facilities). Drives applicability of subsequent standards.

title: Security Management Controls
text: Documented + implemented cyber security policies, exceptions process, designated CIP Senior Manager, designation of delegates. R1 R2 R3 R4. For low-impact: physical security, electronic access control, cyber security awareness, incident response.

title: Personnel & Training
text: Cyber security awareness program (R1), cyber security training (R2), personnel risk assessments (R3 — 7-year criminal-history check at hire + every 7 years), access management program (R4), access revocation (R5).

title: Electronic Security Perimeters
text: R1: establish + maintain ESP for each BES Cyber System. R1.1 all assets within ESP, R1.2 all External Routable Connectivity through identified Electronic Access Points (EAPs), R1.3 EAP rules deny-by-default, R2 Interactive Remote Access through Intermediate System with multi-factor auth + encryption.

title: Physical Security of BES Cyber Systems
text: R1 PSP with physical access controls, R2 PSP with monitoring, R3 visitor management (escort, log retain 90 days).

title: System Security Management
text: R1 ports + services (deny all not necessary), R2 security patch management (35-day evaluation, 35-day implementation OR mitigation plan), R3 malicious code prevention, R4 security event monitoring, R5 system access control (auth, default-account changes, password policies).

title: Incident Reporting + Response Planning
text: R1 documented cyber incident response plan, R2 implementation (within 1 hour notify E-ISAC for Reportable Cyber Security Incident), R3 testing (every 15 calendar months including tabletop or actual incident), R4 retention (5 years).

title: Recovery Plans for BES Cyber Systems
text: R1 recovery plan documented (per Bes Cyber System or shared per ESP), R2 testing (every 15 months including operational exercise every 36 months), R3 maintenance (review after any plan-affecting event).

title: Configuration Change Management + Vulnerability Assessments
text: R1 baseline configuration (operating system, commercial / open-source / custom software, custom + open ports, custom + open security patches), R1.5 monitoring for unauthorized changes, R2 configuration monitoring + investigation of unauthorized changes, R3 vulnerability assessments (every 15 months active testing, every 36 months for new BES Cyber Systems), R4 transient cyber asset + removable media controls.

title: Information Protection
text: R1 documented information protection program for BES Cyber System Information (BCSI), R2 reuse + disposal procedures.

title: Supply Chain Risk Management
text: R1 documented supply chain cyber security risk management plan for procurement of BES Cyber Systems. R2 implementation. Vendor risk identification, evaluation, software integrity validation, vendor remote-access monitoring.

title: Physical Security of Critical Substations
text: Identifies critical transmission facilities and requires risk-informed physical security plans. R1 risk-assessment of transmission stations / substations, R2 third-party verification, R3 evaluation of threats + vulnerabilities, R4 physical security plan, R5 third-party review of plan, R6 implementation.

title: Impact tier matrix
text: Applicability summary: High Impact → all CIP-002 to CIP-013 + CIP-014. Medium Impact → most CIP requirements with some technical exceptions. Low Impact → CIP-002 + select CIP-003 subset only (no asset-level baseline, no detailed config monitoring). Tier determines compliance burden.
